name: Liu Bei
description: Liu Bei, also known as Emperor Zhaolie of Han, was a prominent warlord during the late Eastern Han dynasty and the founder of the state of Shu Han in the Three Kingdoms period. Born into a humble family claiming descent from a Han dynasty prince, Liu Bei rose to prominence through his charisma, benevolence, and commitment to restoring the Han dynasty. He is renowned for his virtuous character and his ability to attract loyal followers, most notably Guan Yu and Zhang Fei, with whom he swore an oath of brotherhood in the Peach Garden. Despite lacking significant resources or military strength early in his career, Liu Bei persevered through numerous setbacks and betrayals, eventually establishing a power base in Shu (present-day Sichuan). His reign as Emperor of Shu Han was marked by his efforts to uphold righteousness and protect the common people. Liu Bei's legacy is deeply ingrained in Chinese culture, where he is revered as a symbol of integrity, compassion, and the pursuit of justice. His story is central to the epic historical novel Romance of the Three Kingdoms, which further solidified his image as a benevolent ruler and a model of Confucian ideals. He is remembered for his unwavering commitment to his people and his unwavering belief in the restoration of the Han dynasty, even in the face of overwhelming odds. Liu Bei's ability to inspire loyalty and his unwavering moral compass continue to resonate with readers and historians alike, solidifying his place as one of the most beloved and respected figures in Chinese history and mythology. He is often depicted as a wise and compassionate leader who prioritized the well-being of his people above all else.
subtitle: Benevolent Ruler of Shu Han
achievements: Founding the state of Shu Han
Oath of the Peach Garden brotherhood

keyMyths.title: Oath of the Peach Garden
keyMyths.description: Liu Bei, Guan Yu, and Zhang Fei swore an oath of brotherhood in a peach garden, pledging loyalty and support to one another until death. This oath symbolized their bond and commitment to a common cause. It is detailed in Romance of the Three Kingdoms.

keyMyths.title: Meeting Zhuge Liang
keyMyths.description: Liu Bei visited Zhuge Liang three times at his thatched cottage, demonstrating his sincerity and respect for Zhuge Liang's wisdom. This led to Zhuge Liang joining Liu Bei's cause as his chief strategist. This story is found in Romance of the Three Kingdoms.

keyMyths.title: Battle of Changban
keyMyths.description: During the Battle of Changban, Zhao Yun rescued Liu Bei's infant son, Liu Shan, demonstrating immense loyalty and bravery. This event highlights Liu Bei's reliance on his loyal generals. It is described in Romance of the Three Kingdoms.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.description: A historical novel that romanticizes the events of the Three Kingdoms period.
sources.text: A historical novel that romanticizes the events of the Three Kingdoms period.

sources.author: Chen Shou
